 {"id":"bd-1nov","title":"Task: Add recovery config options","description":"## Purpose
Add configuration options for controlling session recovery behavior.

## Config Section
[recovery]
enabled = true                    # Master toggle
include_agent_mail = true         # Include recent messages
include_cm_memories = true        # Include procedural memories
include_beads_context = true      # Include BV task status
max_recovery_tokens = 2000        # Cap recovery context size
auto_inject_on_spawn = true       # Send automatically
stale_threshold_hours = 24        # Ignore context older than this

## Scope
- Add RecoveryConfig struct to internal/config/
- Parse from config.toml and environment variables
- Document options in example config
- Support per-session overrides via flags

## Acceptance Criteria
- All options configurable via config file
- Environment variable overrides work
- --no-recovery flag disables for single spawn
- Defaults are sensible (all enabled)

## Technical Notes

 {"id":"bd-1nqde","title":"Task: Parity Gate CI (kernel coverage + CLI/REST parity)","description":"## Scope\nAdd CI tests that prevent drift between CLI, robot mode, and REST/OpenAPI.\n\n## Implementation\n- Validate every kernel command has CLI + REST bindings.\n- Ensure every CLI command is registered in kernel.\n- Compare generated OpenAPI vs checked‑in spec (fail on diff).\n- CLI vs REST parity tests for representative commands.\n\n## Logging\n- Log missing bindings/commands with exact IDs and paths.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: kernel coverage tests.\n- Integration: parity harness comparing normalized CLI JSON vs REST JSON.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- CI fails on missing bindings or OpenAPI drift.\n- Parity tests catch output mismatches deterministically.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:49:37.823284186Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T20:24:55.757982074Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T20:24:55.757424424Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nqde","depends_on_id":"bd-204ic","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nqde","depends_on_id":"bd-vmwdj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ny8s","title":"Task: Write E2E tests for ensemble Robot API","description":"# Task: Robot API E2E Tests

End-to-end tests for ensemble robot commands with full JSON validation.

## Coverage
- --robot-ensemble
- --robot-ensemble-spawn
- --robot-ensemble-synthesize
- --robot-ensemble-modes (default core)
- --robot-ensemble-modes --tier=all (includes advanced)
- --robot-ensemble-modes --category=Formal (filter)
- --robot-ensemble-modes --limit/--offset (pagination)
- --robot-ensemble-presets (list)
- **advanced gating**: spawn with advanced mode without `--allow-advanced` (expect error), then with `--allow-advanced` (expect success)

## Logging Requirements
- Log raw JSON output
- Log parsed response structs
- Log session name + agent counts
- Log tier counts (core vs advanced)
- Log error_code + hint for gating failures

-{"id":"bd-1o1q1","title":"RCH: Add --robot-rch-status and --robot-rch-workers commands","description":"Add robot mode commands for RCH status and worker information.

## Command: --robot-rch-status

```bash
ntm --robot-rch-status
```

### Output Schema

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "rch": {
    "enabled": true,
    "available": true,
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "workers": {
      "total": 3,
      "healthy": 2,
      "busy": 1
    },
    "session_stats": {
      "builds_total": 45,
      "builds_remote": 30,
      "builds_local": 15,
      "time_saved_seconds": 1200
    }
  }
}
```

## Command: --robot-rch-workers

```bash
ntm --robot-rch-workers [--worker=NAME]
```

### Output Schema

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "workers": [
    {
      "name": "builder-1",
      "host": "192.168.1.100",
      "status": "healthy",
      "current_build": null,
      "builds_completed": 20,
      "cpu_percent": 15
    },
    {
      "name": "builder-2",
      "host": "192.168.1.101",
      "status": "busy",
      "current_build": "cargo build --release",
      "builds_completed": 25,
      "cpu_percent": 95
    }
  ]
}
```

## Error Response

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "rch not found in PATH",
  "hint": "Install rch: cargo install rch"
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/robot/rch_status.go
- internal/robot/rch_workers.go

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--robot-rch-status` flag registered in root.go
- [ ] `--robot-rch-workers` flag registered in root.go
- [ ] `--worker=NAME` filter works for specific worker
- [ ] JSON output matches schema exactly
- [ ] Missing rch returns actionable error with install hint
- [ ] Workers array includes all configured workers
- [ ] session_stats aggregates from current session
- [ ] Exit code 0 on success, 1 on partial data, 2 on error

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (internal/robot/rch_status_test.go)
- [ ] Flag parsing for all options
- [ ] rch status parsing
- [ ] rch workers parsing
- [ ] JSON output serialization
- [ ] Error handling for missing rch
- [ ] Error handling for unreachable workers

### E2E Tests (scripts/e2e_robot_rch.sh)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-RCH] Robot RCH Status Tests
set -euo pipefail
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-RCH]"

echo "$LOG_PREFIX Starting robot RCH E2E tests"

# Check if rch is available
if \! command -v rch &>/dev/null; then
    echo "$LOG_PREFIX SKIP: rch not installed"
    exit 0
fi

# Test 1: RCH status
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 1: RCH status"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-rch-status)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".success") == "true" ]] || exit 1
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".rch.version") \!= "" ]] || exit 1

# Test 2: RCH workers
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 2: RCH workers"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-rch-workers)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".success") == "true" ]] || exit 1
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq ".workers | type") == "\"array\"" ]] || exit 1

# Test 3: Missing rch fallback (if applicable)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 3: Error handling"
# Mock test - verify error schema
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Error schema verified"

echo "$LOG_PREFIX All robot RCH E2E tests passed"

 {"id":"bd-1o2r","title":"Fix double-width character handling (emoji, CJK)","description":"## Problem
Double-width characters (emoji, CJK, some Unicode) cause column misalignment because they take 2 terminal cells but count as 1 rune.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Good News: Already Handled Correctly

**The codebase uses `lipgloss.Width()` which internally uses `go-runewidth`:**

1. **table.go:205-210** - `runeWidth()` uses `lipgloss.Width()`:
```go
// runeWidth returns the visual display width of a string.
// Uses lipgloss.Width() which properly handles ANSI escape codes and
// double-width characters (CJK, emoji) that occupy 2 terminal columns.
func runeWidth(s string) int {
    return lipgloss.Width(s)
}
```

2. **layout.go:93-128** - `TruncateWidth()` uses `lipgloss.Width()`:
```go
// TruncateWidth trims a string to fit within maxWidth terminal columns,
// ...Unlike TruncateRunes, this uses lipgloss.Width()
// to properly account for double-width characters (CJK, emoji) and ANSI codes.
func TruncateWidth(s string, maxWidth int, suffix string) string {
    currentWidth := lipgloss.Width(s)
    ...
}
```

3. **badges.go:687-712** - truncation uses `lipgloss.Width()`:
```go
// Uses lipgloss.Width() to properly handle ANSI codes and double-width runes.
```

### Non-Width Uses Are Correct

Found `utf8.RuneCountInString()` used in:
- **activity.go:81-82** - Counting characters for velocity metrics (chars/sec)
- **palette/model.go:1279** - Displaying "X chars" badge

Both are for CHARACTER COUNTING, not display width. Using `RuneCountInString` is correct there.

## Verification Tests Required

Add tests to verify double-width handling:

```go
func TestDoubleWidthCharacterWidth(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name     string
        input    string
        wantWidth int
    }{
        {"ASCII", "hello", 5},
        {"Emoji single", "😀", 2},
        {"Emoji sequence", "👨‍👩‍👧‍👦", 2},  // Family emoji (ZWJ sequence)
        {"CJK", "中文", 4},
        {"Mixed", "hi中文!", 7},
        {"Checkmark", "✓", 1},
        {"Warning", "⚠", 1},
        {"Full block", "█", 1},
    }
    
    for _, tc := range tests {
        t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            got := lipgloss.Width(tc.input)
            if got != tc.wantWidth {
                t.Errorf("Width(%q) = %d, want %d", tc.input, got, tc.wantWidth)
            }
        })
    }
}

func TestTableAlignmentWithEmoji(t *testing.T) {
    table := NewStyledTable("Status", "Name", "Count")
    table.AddRow("✓", "Test 1", "10")
    table.AddRow("⚠", "Test 2 中文", "20")
    table.AddRow("✗", "Test 😀 3", "30")
    
    rendered := table.Render()
    lines := strings.Split(rendered, "\n")
    
    // All lines should have same width (accounting for borders)
    var widths []int
    for _, line := range lines {
        if strings.TrimSpace(line) != "" {
            widths = append(widths, lipgloss.Width(line))
        }
    }
    
    for i := 1; i < len(widths); i++ {
        if widths[i] != widths[0] {
            t.Errorf("line %d width %d != first line width %d", i, widths[i], widths[0])
        }
    }
}

func TestTruncateWidthWithEmoji(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        input    string
        maxWidth int
        want     string
    }{
        {"hello😀world", 8, "hello😀…"},  // emoji is 2 wide
        {"中文测试", 5, "中文…"},          // CJK truncation
        {"test中", 5, "test…"},           // Mixed truncation
    }
    
    for _, tc := range tests {
        got := layout.TruncateWidth(tc.input, tc.maxWidth, "…")
        gotWidth := lipgloss.Width(got)
        if gotWidth > tc.maxWidth {
            t.Errorf("TruncateWidth(%q, %d) = %q (width %d), exceeds max", 
                tc.input, tc.maxWidth, got, gotWidth)
        }
    }
}
```

## E2E Visual Test (VHS)
```tape
# double_width_test.tape
Output double_width_test.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200

Type "ntm --demo-mode"
Sleep 2s

# The demo should show agents with emoji icons
# Verify alignment visually
Screenshot double_width_check.png
```

## Status: Verification Only

The implementation is already correct. This bead becomes a verification/documentation task:
- [x] runeWidth() uses lipgloss.Width()
- [x] TruncateWidth() uses lipgloss.Width() 
- [x] Non-width uses are appropriate (char counting)
- [ ] Add unit tests for double-width scenarios
- [ ] Verify in VHS recording

## Priority Adjustment  
Changed to P3 - verification task.  {"id":"bd-2g6y","title":"Add NO_COLOR and TERM=dumb support for accessible plain mode","description":"## Problem
Plain theme may still show colors; need full NO_COLOR compliance.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Good News: NO_COLOR Already Implemented

**theme.go:278-299** already handles NO_COLOR:
```go
// Respects the NO_COLOR standard (https://no-color.org/):
// - If NO_COLOR exists in environment (any value), colors are disabled
// - NTM_NO_COLOR=1 also disables colors
// - NTM_NO_COLOR=0 forces colors ON (overrides NO_COLOR)
func NoColorEnabled() bool {
    ntmNoColor := strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv("NTM_NO_COLOR"))
    ...
    _, noColorSet := os.LookupEnv("NO_COLOR")
    ...
}
```

**output/*.go** files also check NO_COLOR:
- text.go:189
- success.go:43
- errors.go:58
- confirm.go:53
- progress.go:56,201

### Missing: TERM=dumb Support

No handling found for TERM=dumb. This should be added:
```go
func NoColorEnabled() bool {
    // Existing NO_COLOR checks...

    // Add TERM=dumb check
    if os.Getenv("TERM") == "dumb" {
        return true
    }

    return noColorSet
}
```

### Missing: ASCII Fallback Verification

Need to verify Plain theme uses ASCII-only characters:
- Box drawing: +-| instead of Unicode
- Status icons: [OK] [FAIL] instead of ✓ ✗

## Fix Implementation

### 1. Add TERM=dumb Check
```go
// theme.go
func NoColorEnabled() bool {
    // ... existing checks ...

    // TERM=dumb should also disable colors
    if os.Getenv("TERM") == "dumb" {
        return true
    }

    return noColorSet
}
```

### 2. Verify ASCII Icon Fallback
```go
// icons/icons.go
func asciiIcons() IconSet {
    return IconSet{
        Success: "[OK]",
        Error:   "[ERR]",
        Warning: "[WARN]",
        Info:    "[INFO]",
        // ... all ASCII
    }
}
```

## Unit Tests Required
```go
func TestTermDumbDisablesColor(t *testing.T) {
    t.Setenv("TERM", "dumb")
    t.Setenv("NTM_NO_COLOR", "")

    if !NoColorEnabled() {
        t.Error("TERM=dumb should enable no-color mode")
    }
}

func TestPlainThemeNoANSI(t *testing.T) {
    theme.SetCurrent(theme.Plain)

    styles := NewSemanticStyles(theme.Current())
    rendered := styles.TextSuccess.Render("test")

    if strings.Contains(rendered, "\x1b[") {
        t.Error("Plain theme should not contain ANSI escape codes")
    }
}

func TestPlainThemeASCIIOnly(t *testing.T) {
    theme.SetCurrent(theme.Plain)
    icons := icons.Current()

    // All icons should be ASCII
    for name, icon := range icons.All() {
        for _, r := range icon {
            if r > 127 {
                t.Errorf("icon %s contains non-ASCII character: %c", name, r)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

-{"id":"bd-2gnp3","title":"rano: Implement --robot-rano-stats command","description":"## Overview

Add `ntm --robot-rano-stats` command for per-agent network statistics via rano integration.

## IMPORTANT: API Design Consistency

This command follows the established robot mode patterns:
- **Naming**: `--robot-rano-stats` (tool prefix: rano)
- **NOT** `--robot-network-stats` (must include tool name)
- **Modifiers**: shared `--panes=` flag

## Command Interface

```bash
# Get network stats for all agents
ntm --robot-rano-stats

# Filter to specific pane
ntm --robot-rano-stats --panes=2,3

# Custom time window
ntm --robot-rano-stats --rano-window=10m
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "window": "5m",
  "agents": {
    "pane_2": {
      "connections": 3,
      "bytes_sent": 45000,
      "bytes_received": 120000,
      "requests_estimate": 15,
      "providers": {
        "anthropic": {
          "connections": 2,
          "bytes_sent": 40000,
          "bytes_received": 100000
        }
      },
      "avg_latency_ms": 250
    }
  },
  "total": {
    "connections": 4,
    "bytes_sent": 55000,
    "bytes_received": 170000
  }
}
```

## Error Response

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "rano not found in PATH",
  "hint": "Install rano: go install github.com/.../rano@latest"
}
```

## Implementation

1. Check rano availability via ToolRegistry
2. Get PID map for all/specified panes via tmux
3. Call rano with PIDs: `rano --pids=PID1,PID2 --window=DURATION --json`
4. Aggregate stats by pane
5. Format as JSON

## Modifier Flags

| Flag | Description | Default |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `--panes=N` | Filter to specific pane(s) | All agent panes |
| `--rano-window=DURATION` | Time window for stats | 5m |

## Code Location
- internal/robot/rano_stats.go

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--robot-rano-stats` flag registered in root.go
- [ ] `--panes=N` filter works with comma-separated pane IDs
- [ ] `--rano-window=DURATION` accepts Go duration format (5m, 1h, etc.)
- [ ] JSON output matches schema exactly
- [ ] Missing rano returns actionable error with install hint
- [ ] PID-to-pane mapping correctly attributes traffic
- [ ] Provider breakdown (anthropic/openai/google) works
- [ ] Exit code 0 on success, 1 on partial data, 2 on error

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (internal/robot/rano_stats_test.go)
- [ ] Flag parsing for all options
- [ ] PID-to-pane mapping logic
- [ ] rano output parsing
- [ ] Provider aggregation
- [ ] JSON output serialization
- [ ] Error handling for missing rano
- [ ] Error handling for rano failures

### E2E Tests (scripts/e2e_robot_rano.sh)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-RANO] Robot Rano Stats Tests
set -euo pipefail
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-RANO]"

echo "$LOG_PREFIX Starting robot rano E2E tests"

# Check if rano is available
if \! command -v rano &>/dev/null; then
    echo "$LOG_PREFIX SKIP: rano not installed"
    exit 0
fi

# Setup: Create test session
TEST_SESSION="e2e_rano_$$"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Creating test session: $TEST_SESSION"
ntm spawn "$TEST_SESSION" --cc=1 --no-prompt
sleep 5

# Test 1: Basic stats
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 1: Basic rano stats"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-rano-stats)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".success") == "true" ]] || exit 1
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".timestamp") \!= "" ]] || exit 1

# Test 2: Pane filter
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 2: Pane-filtered stats"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-rano-stats --panes=1)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".success") == "true" ]] || exit 1

# Test 3: Custom window
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 3: Custom time window"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-rano-stats --rano-window=1m)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".window") == "1m" ]] || exit 1

# Cleanup
tmux kill-session -t "$TEST_SESSION" 2>/dev/null || true

echo "$LOG_PREFIX All robot rano E2E tests passed"
```","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:15:41.491769242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:04:41.099661256Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gnp3","depends_on_id":"bd-1xxhe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2gnp3","depends_on_id":"bd-27l8q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2gnp3","depends_on_id":"bd-308f5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2gnp3","depends_on_id":"bd-33wzo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2gp6","title":"Fix ANSI escape code width calculation errors","description":"## Problem
Width calculations not properly accounting for ANSI escape codes, causing misalignment and overflow.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

**Good news: The codebase already handles ANSI width correctly in most places.**

### Already Correct:
- `internal/cli/table.go:208` - `runeWidth()` uses `lipgloss.Width(s)`
- `internal/tui/layout/layout.go:102` - `TruncateWidth()` uses `lipgloss.Width()`
- `internal/tui/styles/badges.go:687` - Documents: "Uses lipgloss.Width() to properly handle ANSI codes"
- `internal/tui/dashboard/panels/ticker.go` - Builds plain text first, then styles visible portion
- 36+ uses of `lipgloss.Width()` found across the codebase

### Remaining Audit Needed
Verify no edge cases where `len()` is used on strings that might already be styled:
1. Dashboard panel rendering code
2. Card grid layout calculations  
3. Status line construction
4. Any dynamic width calculations based on content

## Updated Fix Approach
1. **Audit** all width calculations - verify they use lipgloss.Width() or operate on plain text
2. **Add tests** to verify styled string width handling
3. **Document** the correct pattern in CONTRIBUTING.md

## Unit Tests Required
```go
func TestANSIWidthCalculations(t *testing.T) {
    // Verify lipgloss.Width() correctly ignores ANSI codes
    plain := "Hello"
    styled := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("212")).Render("Hello")
    
    if lipgloss.Width(styled) != lipgloss.Width(plain) {
        t.Errorf("styled width %d != plain width %d", lipgloss.Width(styled), lipgloss.Width(plain))
    }
    
    // Verify runeWidth() uses lipgloss.Width()
    w := runeWidth(styled)
    if w != 5 {
        t.Errorf("runeWidth(styled) = %d, want 5", w)
    }
}

func TestGradientTextWidth(t *testing.T) {
    text := "Test"
    gradient := styles.GradientText(text, "#FF0000", "#00FF00")
    
    // Gradient text should have same display width as original
    if lipgloss.Width(gradient) != 4 {
        t.Errorf("gradient width = %d, want 4", lipgloss.Width(gradient))
    }
}

func TestTableColumnAlignment(t *testing.T) {
    table := NewStyledTable("Name", "Value")
    // Add rows with styled content
    table.AddRow(lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Render("Bold"), "plain")
    table.AddRow("plain", lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("212")).Render("colored"))
    
    rendered := table.Render()
    lines := strings.Split(rendered, "\n")
    
    // All lines should have consistent width (no jagged edges)
    var widths []int
    for _, line := range lines {
        if strings.TrimSpace(line) != "" {
            widths = append(widths, lipgloss.Width(line))
        }
    }
    
    // Check all widths are equal (within tolerance for borders)
    for i := 1; i < len(widths); i++ {
        if widths[i] != widths[0] {
            t.Errorf("line %d width %d != first line width %d", i, widths[i], widths[0])
        }
    }
}
```

## Verification
- Run audit to confirm no `len()` used on styled strings for width
- All styled text aligns correctly in tables
- Gradient text does not cause overflow
- Unit tests pass

## Priority Adjustment
Changed from P1 to P2 - most code is already correct, this is mainly an audit/verification task.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:09:28.352073676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T08:59:12.711902846Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T08:59:12.711841760Z","close_reason":"Audit complete: no len() on styled strings found.  {"id":"bd-2h4bq","title":"Task: Define robot output envelope + error taxonomy","description":"## Overview
Robot output envelope already exists (`internal/robot/types.go`). This task **audits and formalizes** it, then extends it for multi‑format output (JSON/TOON) and verbosity profiles.

## Current Reality (must stay aligned)
`RobotResponse` already defines:
- `success`
- `timestamp` (RFC3339 UTC)
- `error`
- `error_code`
- `hint`

Error codes already include:
`SESSION_NOT_FOUND`, `PANE_NOT_FOUND`, `INVALID_FLAG`, `TIMEOUT`, `NOT_IMPLEMENTED`, `DEPENDENCY_MISSING`, `INTERNAL_ERROR`, `PERMISSION_DENIED`, `RESOURCE_BUSY`.

## Requirements
- Required fields present on all success responses (`success`, `timestamp`).
- Critical arrays are **always present** (empty `[]` if none), per AGENTS JSON semantics.
- Optional meta fields (`_agent_hints`, `warnings`, `notes`) remain optional.
- Same field names across JSON/TOON (no renames).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Updated docs/spec that match `RobotResponse` (no divergence).
- Lint/test coverage to ensure envelope fields present on all robot responses.
- Clear guidance for new robot commands on required vs optional fields.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:13:52.715416697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:11:59.411239045Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:11:59.411164795Z","close_reason":"Completed robot output envelope specification:

1. Envelope Spec Documentation (types.go lines 1-65) - comprehensive spec for robot output envelope with required fields, error fields, array requirements, and clear guidance for new output types.

2. Compliance Audit - 22 compliant types that embed RobotResponse, 18 non-compliant types documented for migration.

3. Test Coverage (envelope_test.go ~250 lines) - TestEnvelopeSpec, TestOutputTypesEmbedRobotResponse, TestOutputTypesHaveRequiredJSONTags, TestArrayFieldsNeverNull, TestEnvelope_ErrorCodes.

4. Bug Fix - Fixed pre-existing function name collisions in spawn_test.go.

All tests pass. Unblocks bd-2q3cg, bd-1fmug, bd-4uecx.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2h4bq","depends_on_id":"bd-2dovu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
